news.title: Spain's World Cup triumph ends with players receiving their body weight in tomatoes
news.lede: Spain's World Cup triumph has brought medals, global recognition and record prize money, but for Fabián Ruiz and Gavi, one of the most memorable rewards arrived hundreds of miles from New York New Jersey Stadium. Football News: After winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Spain's Fabián Ruiz and Gavi received a unique hometown honor: their body weight in tomatoes during a civic celebration in Los Palacios y Villafranca. This tradition marks a quirky yet heartfelt tribute to local football heroes following their historic triumph.

news.title: MatchWornShirt auctions Luka Modrić shirt from his final World Cup win
news.lede: MatchWornShirt has launched an exclusive auction offering football fans and collectors the opportunity to own one of the most significant pieces of modern international football memorabilia: Luka Modrić's match-worn and signed home shirt from Croatia's 2-1 victory over Ghana, marking the final FIFA World Cup victory of his iconic career with the Vatreni. The auction opens today and runs until 31st July, giving supporters and collectors worldwide the chance to bid on a shirt worn by one of the game's greatest midfielders during what is expected to be his final FIFA World Cup appearance. Widely regarded as Croatia's greatest-ever player, Modrić has defined an era for both club and country. During his illustrious spell at Real Madrid, he became one of the most decorated midfielders in football history, winning multiple UEFA Champions League titles and the 2018 Ballon d'Or.

news.title: Ismail Elfath: Morocco Has What It Takes to Fight for the 2030 World Cup
news.lede: Casablanca – Moroccan- born American referee Ismail Elfath believes Morocco has what it takes to challenge for the FIFA World Cup title in 2030, saying the Atlas Lions' recent success is the result of years of planning rather than a surprise rise. Speaking in an interview with Saudi newspaper Arriyadiyah, Elfath praised Morocco's long-term football project following the national team's strong performances at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. "The results Morocco are achieving are no longer surprising," Ellfath said. "They are the product of work that began more than ten years ago through a clear vision focused on investing in infrastructure and developing talent."

news.title: Norway considers filing Fifa ethics complaint over Trump role in World Cup red card saga
news.lede: Norwegian federation president: Fifa on a ‘slippery slope’. Trump called Infantino for review of Balogun’s red. Donald Trump’s role in clearing Folarin Balogun from a mandatory one-game ban at the World Cup is set to provoke a formal complaint to Fifa ethics investigators. The Norway soccer federation said Thursday its board of directors will consider filing the complaint at its next meeting, likely on 6 August.

news.title: Domestic abuse reports reached record highs in the UK during the 2026 World Cup, figures have shown.
news.lede: In total, 384 domestic violence incidents were reported during the tournament - representing 17 per cent of the 2,322 incidents reported. Ninety-three per cent occurred around England's matches and only Euro 2020 (held in 2021) recorded more. The rise has been described as a worrying increase, with alcohol once again viewed as the lead contributing factor. "The vast majority of it [incidents] we'd say were alcohol related and that's a real concern," Chief Constable Mark Roberts, the Football Policing National Lead in charge of policing football in the UK, told Sky Sports News .

news.title: 82,000 Argentina fans sign petition calling for World Cup Final replay over referee controversy
news.lede: Thousands of furious Argentina supporters are calling for the FIFA World Cup Final to be replayed following what they describe as a "corrupt" officiating display. Argentina fell 1-0 to Spain in extra time of the tournament showpiece in New Jersey on Sunday. Ferran Torres' strike proved decisive for the commanding Spanish side, who relentlessly pressured the Argentina goal throughout the game. Defending champions Argentina failed to register a single shot on target in regulation and were reduced to 10 men after Enzo Fernández received a red card in the 93rd minute.
